title: FIREWALL RULE ENGINE v3.1
description:
== FIREWALL RULE ENGINE v3.1 ==
STATUS: WIPED BY [NULL]

[NULL] erased the firewall's rule engine.
Packets flow unchecked. Every rule was a single encoded string.
Parse the format, enforce the logic. One misread and the breach widens.

RULE FORMAT:
    "ACTION|port_min-port_max|protocol"
    ACTION is either "ALLOW" or "DENY"
    port_min and port_max define an inclusive range
    protocol is a string like "TCP" or "UDP"

PACKET FORMAT:
    "port|protocol"

EVALUATION LOGIC:
    1. Parse the packet's port as an integer and its protocol as a string.
    2. Parse the rule's action, port range, and protocol.
    3. If the packet's port falls within the range (inclusive)
       AND the protocol matches (case-insensitive), return the action.
    4. Otherwise, return "NO_MATCH".

title: FIREWALL RULE ENGINE v3.2
description:
== FIREWALL RULE ENGINE v3.2 ==
STATUS: WIPED BY [NULL]

[NULL] erased the firewall's IP-based rule engine.
Traffic flows unfiltered. Every rule was a single encoded string.
Parse the format, enforce the logic. One wrong IP and the whole floor is exposed.

RULE FORMAT:
    "ACTION|ip_prefix|port"
    ACTION is either "ALLOW" or "DENY"
    ip_prefix is a partial IP like "192.168" (match if packet IP starts with this)
    port is an exact port number

PACKET FORMAT:
    "ip_address|port"

EVALUATION LOGIC:
    1. Parse the packet's IP address (string) and port (integer).
    2. Parse the rule's action, IP prefix, and port.
    3. If the packet's IP starts with the rule's ip_prefix
       AND the port matches exactly, return the action.
    4. Otherwise, return "NO_MATCH".

title: FIREWALL RULE ENGINE v3.3
description:
== FIREWALL RULE ENGINE v3.3 ==
STATUS: WIPED BY [NULL]

[NULL] erased the outbound traffic filter.
Every packet escapes unchecked. Rules encoded as single strings are all that remain.
Parse the format, enforce the logic. The building leaks data with every second.

RULE FORMAT:
    "ACTION|protocol|min_port"
    ACTION is either "ALLOW" or "DENY"
    protocol is a string like "TCP", "UDP", "ICMP"
    min_port is a threshold: rule applies to packets with port >= min_port

PACKET FORMAT:
    "protocol|port"

EVALUATION LOGIC:
    1. Parse the packet's protocol (string) and port (integer).
    2. Parse the rule's action, protocol, and minimum port.
    3. If the protocol matches (case-insensitive)
       AND the packet's port >= min_port, return the action.
    4. Otherwise, return "NO_MATCH".

title: FIREWALL RULE ENGINE v3.4
description:
== FIREWALL RULE ENGINE v3.4 ==
STATUS: WIPED BY [NULL]

[NULL] erased the zone-based packet filter.
Internal zones bleed into external. Every rule was a single encoded string.
Parse the format, enforce the logic. One wrong zone and the core is exposed.

RULE FORMAT:
    "ACTION|zone|port_min-port_max"
    ACTION is either "ALLOW" or "DENY"
    zone is a string like "INTERNAL", "EXTERNAL", "DMZ"
    port_min and port_max define an inclusive range

PACKET FORMAT:
    "zone|port"

EVALUATION LOGIC:
    1. Parse the packet's zone (string) and port (integer).
    2. Parse the rule's action, zone, and port range.
    3. If the zone matches (case-insensitive)
       AND the port falls within the range (inclusive), return the action.
    4. Otherwise, return "NO_MATCH".

title: DYNAMIC MEMORY ALLOCATOR v2.0
description:
== DYNAMIC MEMORY ALLOCATOR v2.0 ==
STATUS: CHAIN SEVERED BY [NULL]

[NULL] corrupted the dynamic memory allocator's linked structure.
Nodes point nowhere. The chain is severed at every link.
Rebuild the node definition and the reversal engine.

REQUIREMENTS:
    Define a static inner class "Node" inside MemoryAllocator with:
        - an int field "data"
        - a Node field "next"
        - a constructor that takes an int and sets next to null

    Then write a static method:
        public static String buildAndReverse(int[] values)

    This method must:
        1. Build a singly linked list from the array (first element = head).
        2. Reverse the entire linked list in-place using pointer manipulation.
        3. Return the reversed list as a string: "data1 -> data2 -> data3"
        4. Return "EMPTY" if the input array has no elements.

title: DYNAMIC MEMORY ALLOCATOR v2.1
description:
== DYNAMIC MEMORY ALLOCATOR v2.1 ==
STATUS: CORRUPTED BY [NULL]

[NULL] destroyed the memory chain analyzer.
Length is unknown. The midpoint is lost.
Rebuild the node structure, count the chain, and locate the center.

REQUIREMENTS:
    Define a static inner class "Node" inside MemoryAllocator with:
        - an int field "data"
        - a Node field "next"
        - a constructor that takes an int and sets next to null

    Then write a static method:
        public static String buildAndFindMiddle(int[] values)

    This method must:
        1. Build a singly linked list from the array (first element = head).
        2. Find the middle node using slow/fast pointer technique.
           For even-length lists, return the second of the two middle nodes.
        3. Return "LENGTH:n|MIDDLE:data" where n is total nodes.
        4. Return "EMPTY" if the input array has no elements.

title: DYNAMIC MEMORY ALLOCATOR v2.2
description:
== DYNAMIC MEMORY ALLOCATOR v2.2 ==
STATUS: POLLUTED BY [NULL]

[NULL] injected duplicate entries into the memory chain.
Repeated allocations corrupt the heap. Duplicates must be purged.
Rebuild the chain and strip all duplicate values, keeping only first occurrences.

REQUIREMENTS:
    Define a static inner class "Node" inside MemoryAllocator with:
        - an int field "data"
        - a Node field "next"
        - a constructor that takes an int and sets next to null

    Then write a static method:
        public static String buildAndDeduplicate(int[] values)

    This method must:
        1. Build a singly linked list from the array.
        2. Remove all duplicate values, keeping only the FIRST occurrence.
        3. Return the deduplicated list as "data1 -> data2 -> data3".
        4. Return "EMPTY" if the input array has no elements.

title: DYNAMIC MEMORY ALLOCATOR v2.3
description:
== DYNAMIC MEMORY ALLOCATOR v2.3 ==
STATUS: INFECTED BY [NULL]

[NULL] planted rogue entries in the memory chain.
A known malicious value is embedded at random positions.
Rebuild the chain and surgically remove every occurrence of the target.

REQUIREMENTS:
    Define a static inner class "Node" inside MemoryAllocator with:
        - an int field "data"
        - a Node field "next"
        - a constructor that takes an int and sets next to null

    Then write a static method:
        public static String buildAndPurge(int[] values, int target)

    This method must:
        1. Build a singly linked list from the array.
        2. Remove ALL nodes whose data equals target.
        3. Return the cleaned list as "data1 -> data2 -> data3".
        4. Return "EMPTY" if the list is empty after purging
           (or if the input array was empty).

title: SYSTEM BOOT SEQUENCER v1.0
description:
== SYSTEM BOOT SEQUENCER v1.0 ==
STATUS: SCRAMBLED BY [NULL]

[NULL] scrambled the boot sequence.
Services start before their dependencies, causing cascade failures.
Reconstruct the topological order. Every dependency must load first.
If a cycle exists, the system is unrecoverable.

INPUT FORMAT:
    Line 1: N (number of services, labeled 0 to N-1)
    Line 2: M (number of dependency pairs)
    Next M lines: "a b" meaning service a depends on service b
                  (b must boot before a)

OUTPUT:
    A single line of space-separated service numbers in valid boot order.
    When multiple services have no pending dependencies,
    boot the lowest-numbered service first.
    If a cycle makes ordering impossible, output "CYCLE_DETECTED".

ALGORITHM HINT:
    Track how many dependencies each service has (in-degree).
    Repeatedly pick the service with zero remaining dependencies,
    boot it, then reduce the count for everything that depends on it.
    If you finish and not all services were booted, there is a cycle.

title: SYSTEM BOOT SEQUENCER v1.1
description:
== SYSTEM BOOT SEQUENCER v1.1 ==
STATUS: INVERTED BY [NULL]

[NULL] reversed the boot priority.
The system now demands highest-numbered services boot first when tied.
Reconstruct the topological order with inverted priority.
If a cycle exists, the system is unrecoverable.

INPUT FORMAT:
    Line 1: N (number of services, labeled 0 to N-1)
    Line 2: M (number of dependency pairs)
    Next M lines: "a b" meaning service a depends on service b
                  (b must boot before a)

OUTPUT:
    A single line of space-separated service numbers in valid boot order.
    When multiple services have no pending dependencies,
    boot the HIGHEST-numbered service first.
    If a cycle makes ordering impossible, output "CYCLE_DETECTED".

EXAMPLES:
    4 services, deps: 1->0, 2->0, 3->2
    Valid: 0 is required first (only zero-indeg node initially).
    Then 1 and 2 are free: pick 2 first (higher). Then 1, then 3.
    Boot order: 0 2 3 1

    3 services, deps: 0->1, 1->2, 2->0
    Output: CYCLE_DETECTED

title: SYSTEM BOOT SEQUENCER v1.2
description:
== SYSTEM BOOT SEQUENCER v1.2 ==
STATUS: DEPTH UNKNOWN BY [NULL]

[NULL] scrambled the boot sequence and erased the layer count.
Without knowing how deep the dependency chain goes, staged rollout is impossible.
Reconstruct the boot order AND report the number of boot layers.
If a cycle exists, the system is unrecoverable.

INPUT FORMAT:
    Line 1: N (number of services, labeled 0 to N-1)
    Line 2: M (number of dependency pairs)
    Next M lines: "a b" meaning service a depends on service b

OUTPUT:
    Line 1: Boot order (space-separated, lowest-first tiebreak).
    Line 2: "LAYERS:X" where X is the number of rounds needed.
            (A round processes all currently available zero-indeg services.)
    If a cycle, output only "CYCLE_DETECTED".

EXAMPLES:
    4 services, deps: 1->0, 2->0, 3->2
    Round 1: boot 0. Round 2: boot 1, 2. Round 3: boot 3.
    Output:
        0 1 2 3
        LAYERS:3

    3 services, deps: 0->1, 1->2, 2->0
    Output: CYCLE_DETECTED

title: SYSTEM SHUTDOWN SEQUENCER v1.0
description:
== SYSTEM SHUTDOWN SEQUENCER v1.0 ==
STATUS: CORRUPTED BY [NULL]

[NULL] scrambled the shutdown sequence.
Services that others depend on are killed first, crashing everything downstream.
Reconstruct the REVERSE topological order: shut down dependents first,
then their dependencies.
If a cycle exists, the system is unrecoverable.

INPUT FORMAT:
    Line 1: N (number of services, labeled 0 to N-1)
    Line 2: M (number of dependency pairs)
    Next M lines: "a b" meaning service a depends on service b
                  (b must boot before a, meaning a must SHUT DOWN before b)

OUTPUT:
    A single line of space-separated service numbers in valid SHUTDOWN order
    (reverse of boot order).
    When multiple services can shut down, pick the LOWEST-numbered first.
    If a cycle, output "CYCLE_DETECTED".

EXAMPLES:
    4 services, deps: 1->0, 2->0, 3->2
    Boot order would be: 0 1 2 3
    Shutdown order (reverse): 3 1 2 0
    (3 and 1 have no dependents; pick 1 first? No: 3 depends on 2 depends on 0,
     1 depends on 0. Shutdown: nodes with no dependents first.
     Out-degree 0: 1, 3. Pick 1 first (lowest). Then 3. Then 2. Then 0.)
    Output: 1 3 2 0

    3 services, deps: 0->1, 1->2, 2->0
    Output: CYCLE_DETECTED
